tools.description: Adds a repository to the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Requires the organization's 'enabled_repositories' policy to be set to 'selected'.

tools.name: Encrypt and update dev secret
tools.description: Creates or updates a repository's development environment secret using an `encrypted_value` and its corresponding `key_id`; the secret must be pre-encrypted with the repository's Codespaces public key.

tools.name: Encrypt org dev env secret
tools.description: Creates or updates an organization's GitHub Codespaces secret using an encrypted value and its corresponding public key ID.

tools.name: Export a codespace for the authenticated user
tools.description: Triggers an export of a user's specified codespace, automatically stopping it if active, and returns its export status and download URL.

tools.name: Export an SBOM for a repository
tools.description: Exports the software bill of materials (SBOM) in SPDX JSON format for a repository, if its dependency graph is enabled and it has at least one commit.

tools.name: Find Pull Requests
tools.description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.

tools.name: Find Repositories
tools.description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.

tools.name: Follow Organization (GraphQL)
tools.description: Tool to follow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow an organization.

tools.name: Follow a user
tools.description: Allows the authenticated user to follow the GitHub user specified by `username`; this action is idempotent and the user cannot follow themselves.

tools.name: Follow User (GraphQL)
tools.description: Tool to follow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow another user.

tools.name: Force cancel a workflow run
tools.description: Forcefully cancels a queued or in-progress GitHub Actions workflow run, bypassing conditions like always() that would otherwise continue execution. Only use when the standard cancel endpoint fails. Cannot cancel completed workflow runs. Requires write permissions to the repository.

tools.name: Fork a gist
tools.description: Forks a specified public gist, creating a copy under the authenticated user's account.

tools.name: Generate release notes content for a release
tools.description: Generates Markdown release notes content (listing changes, pull requests, and contributors) for a GitHub repository release, customizable via tags and a configuration file.

tools.name: Get a blob
tools.description: Retrieves the raw, typically Base64-encoded, content of a file (blob) from a GitHub repository using its SHA hash, if the repository and blob SHA exist.

tools.name: Get a branch
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specified branch within a GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get access restrictions
tools.description: Lists users, teams, and GitHub Apps with push access to a branch; this branch must be protected in repository settings for detailed restrictions, otherwise expect a 404 or empty response.

tools.name: Get a commit
tools.description: Retrieves a specific commit from a repository by its owner, name, and a valid commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag), supporting pagination for large diffs.

tools.name: Get admin branch protection
tools.description: Checks if repository administrators are subject to the branch protection rules on a specific branch.

tools.name: Get all API versions
tools.description: Retrieves all officially supported, date-based (e.g., "2022-11-28") versions of the GitHub REST API from the /versions endpoint.

tools.name: Get all contributor commit activity
tools.description: Retrieves commit activity (total commits, weekly additions/deletions/commits) for all contributors to a repository; may require a retry if GitHub returns 202 while preparing data.

tools.name: Get all deployment protection rules for an environment
tools.description: Lists all enabled custom deployment protection rules for a specific environment in a repository; the environment must exist and be configured for deployments.

tools.name: Get a milestone
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific milestone within a GitHub repository by its number.

tools.name: Get an app
tools.description: Retrieves publicly available information for an existing GitHub App, identified by its unique URL-friendly `app_slug`.

tools.name: Get an artifact
tools.description: Gets a specific artifact for a repository by `artifact_id`. This action retrieves the details of a specific artifact from a GitHub repository using the artifact's unique identifier. It allows users to access information about artifacts stored in the repository.

tools.name: Get an assignment
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub Classroom assignment if the authenticated user is an administrator of the classroom.

tools.name: Get an autolink reference of a repository
tools.description: Retrieves a specific autolink reference (which automatically hyperlinks text like 'JIRA-123' to an external system) for a repository using its unique ID; requires administrator access to the repository.

tools.name: Get an environment
tools.description: Retrieves the details of a specific deployment environment for a given repository, including its name, configurations, and current status.

tools.name: Get an environment public key
tools.description: Retrieves the public key for a specified GitHub repository environment, used to encrypt secrets for GitHub Actions.

tools.name: Get an environment secret
tools.description: Retrieves metadata (name and timestamps) for a single secret in a GitHub Actions environment. Note: The actual secret value is never returned by this API for security reasons.

tools.name: Get an environment variable
tools.description: Retrieves a specific environment variable from a GitHub Actions environment by repository owner, repository name, environment name, and variable name.

tools.name: Get an issue
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific issue in a repository using the owner, repository name, and issue number.

tools.name: Get an issue comment
tools.description: Action to get an issue comment.

tools.name: Get an issue event
tools.description: Fetches details of a specific issue event for a given repository and event ID.

tools.name: Get an organization
tools.description: Get an organization

tools.name: Get an organization public key
tools.description: Action for `GetAnOrganizationPublicKey`.

tools.name: Get an organization role
tools.description: Retrieves a specific GitHub organization role by its ID.

tools.name: Get an organization secret
tools.description: Gets an organization secret's metadata (e.g., name, creation/update dates, visibility), but not its encrypted value.

tools.name: Get an organization variable
tools.description: Retrieves details (name, value, visibility, timestamps) of a specific, existing variable for an existing GitHub organization.

tools.name: Get an organization webhook
tools.description: Retrieves the full configuration, including subscribed events and delivery settings, for an existing organization webhook.

tools.name: Get a package for an organization
tools.description: Retrieves a specific package (by type and name) from an organization, if both the package and organization exist.

tools.name: Get a package for a user
tools.description: Retrieves metadata for a specific package owned by a GitHub user, using package type, name, and username as identifiers.

tools.name: Get a package for the authenticated user
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific package owned by the authenticated user.

tools.name: Get a package version for an organization
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific version of a package within an organization.

tools.name: Get a package version for a user
tools.description: Retrieves a specific public package version associated with a GitHub user.

tools.name: Get authenticated user package version
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for an existing specific package version associated with the authenticated user, identified by its type, name, and version ID.

tools.name: Get a project
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub project (V2) using its project number and owner. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 REST API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed.

tools.name: Get a project card
tools.description: Retrieves all details of a specific project card, given its unique `card_id`. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action only works with existing classic project cards that were created before the sunset date. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead.

tools.name: Get a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific project column from GitHub Projects (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns column details including: id, name, url, project_url, cards_url, node_id, created_at, and updated_at timestamps.

tools.name: Get a pull request
tools.description: Retrieves a specific pull request from a GitHub repository using its owner, repository name, and pull request number.

tools.name: Get a reference
tools.description: Retrieves a specific Git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a GitHub repository. The reference must exist in the repository; use 'list_matching_references' first if unsure what references are available.

tools.name: Get a release
tools.description: Gets a specific release from a GitHub repository, provided the repository is accessible and the release exists.

tools.name: Get a release asset
tools.description: Gets metadata for a specific release asset in a GitHub repository, including a `browser_download_url` for downloading the asset.

tools.name: Get a release by tag name
tools.description: Gets a release from a GitHub repository by its tag name; the repository and a release with this tag must already exist.

tools.name: Get a repository
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about an existing and accessible GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get a repository public key
tools.description: Gets a repository's public key for encrypting secrets to be used in GitHub Actions, if the repository exists and is accessible.

tools.name: Get a repository readme
tools.description: Fetches the README file (if it exists and is accessible) from a specified GitHub repository, returning its Base64-encoded content and metadata.

tools.name: Get a repository readme for a directory
tools.description: Retrieves the README file from a specified directory within a GitHub repository, optionally at a given commit, branch, or tag.

tools.name: Get a repository ruleset
tools.description: Retrieves a specific repository ruleset by its ID; if `includes_parents` is true, the search for this `ruleset_id` also extends to rulesets from parent organizations.

tools.name: Get a repository rule suite
tools.description: Gets detailed information for a specific repository rule suite by its ID, including its evaluation status and the results of its individual rules.

tools.name: Get a repository secret
tools.description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for an existing repository secret, excluding its encrypted value.

tools.name: Get a repository subscription
tools.description: Gets the authenticated user's subscription details for a repository, indicating if they receive notifications.

tools.name: Get a repository variable
tools.description: Gets a specific GitHub Actions variable by name from an accessible repository.

tools.name: Get a repository webhook
tools.description: Returns the configuration of an existing webhook for a given repository.

tools.name: Get a review comment for a pull request
tools.description: Retrieves a specific pull request review comment by its ID, provided the repository exists, is accessible, and the comment ID is valid.

tools.name: Get a review for a pull request
tools.description: Retrieves a specific review for a pull request using its owner, repository, pull request number, and review ID.

tools.name: Get a secret for the authenticated user
tools.description: Retrieves metadata (name, timestamps, visibility; not the value) for a specific, existing development environment secret associated with the authenticated user's GitHub Codespaces.

tools.name: Get a self hosted runner for an organization
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific self-hosted runner registered within a GitHub organization.

tools.name: Get a self hosted runner for a repository
tools.description: Gets a specific self-hosted runner for a repository by its unique ID.

tools.name: Get assignment grades
tools.description: Retrieves all grades for an existing GitHub Classroom assignment.

tools.name: Get a tag
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific Git tag object from a GitHub repository, using the SHA of the tag object itself.

tools.name: Get a team by name
tools.description: Retrieves a GitHub team by its slug from a specific organization.

tools.name: Get a thread
tools.description: Retrieves a specific GitHub notification thread using its unique `thread_id`.

tools.name: Get a thread subscription for the authenticated user
tools.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's subscription details for a specific notification thread, identified by `thread_id`.

tools.name: List repository files and directories
tools.description: Browse and list all files/directories in a GitHub repository. Efficiently retrieves the complete repository structure in a single API call when used with recursive mode. Perfect for analyzing codebase structure, finding specific files, or getting an overview of repository contents without cloning.

tools.name: Get a user
tools.description: Retrieves the public profile information for an existing GitHub user, specified by their username.

tools.name: Get a webhook configuration for an organization
tools.description: Retrieves the configuration for a specific webhook associated with a GitHub organization.

tools.name: Get a webhook configuration for a repository
tools.description: Returns the configuration for an existing webhook on the specified repository.

tools.name: Get a webhook delivery for an organization webhook
tools.description: Returns detailed information for a specific delivery attempt of a webhook configured for the specified GitHub organization.

tools.name: Get a workflow
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub Actions workflow in a repository, identified by either its numeric ID or its filename.

tools.name: Get a workflow run
tools.description: Gets a specific workflow run by its ID from a GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get a workflow run attempt
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific attempt of a workflow run in a GitHub repository, including its status, conclusion, and timings.

tools.name: Get billing usage report for user
tools.description: Retrieves the billing usage report for a specified GitHub user. Use when you need to analyze billing costs and usage patterns.

tools.name: Get branch protection
tools.description: Retrieves branch protection settings for a specific, existing, and accessible branch in a GitHub repository; protection feature availability varies by GitHub product plan.

tools.name: Get catalog models
tools.description: Tool to retrieve the list of AI models available in the GitHub Models catalog. Use when you need to discover available models, their capabilities, token limits, and supported modalities.

tools.name: Get a check run
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific check run within a GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get a check suite
tools.description: Retrieves a specific check suite (a collection of check runs) by its ID from a repository accessible to the authenticated user.

tools.name: Get a classroom
tools.description: Retrieves details for a specific GitHub Classroom. Requires the authenticated user to be an administrator of the classroom.

tools.name: Get a code of conduct
tools.description: Retrieves the full details of a specific GitHub code of conduct using its unique key.

tools.name: Get Code of Conduct (GraphQL)
tools.description: Tool to look up a code of conduct by its key using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to fetch code of conduct details as part of a GraphQL workflow.

tools.name: Get a CodeQL database for a repository
tools.description: Gets an existing CodeQL database (including a download URL) for a specified language in an accessible repository, if one has been successfully built for that language.

tools.name: Get a code scanning alert
tools.description: Retrieves a specific code scanning alert, which identifies potential code vulnerabilities or errors, by its number from the specified GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get a code scanning analysis for a repository
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific code scanning analysis on an accessible repository, identified by its `analysis_id`.

tools.name: Get a code scanning default setup configuration
tools.description: Gets the default setup configuration for code scanning in a repository, including state, languages, query suite, and schedule for a repository if it exists.

tools.name: Get a codespace for the authenticated user
tools.description: Call to retrieve detailed information for a `codespace_name` belonging to the authenticated user, ensuring the codespace exists and is accessible.

tools.name: Get commit authors
tools.description: Fetches commit authors identified during a repository import, used to map authors from an external VCS to GitHub accounts. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

tools.name: Get a commit comment
tools.description: Retrieves the full details of a specific commit comment in a GitHub repository, using its unique identifier.

tools.name: Get a commit object
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information (including author, committer, message, tree, parents, verification) for a specific commit in a GitHub repository, identified by its SHA.

tools.name: Get commit signature protection
tools.description: Gets the commit signature protection status for a branch in a repository.

tools.name: Get commit statuses
tools.description: List all statuses for a commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag) in reverse chronological order. Use when you need to check CI/CD status results from various systems for a specific commit.

tools.name: Get community profile metrics
tools.description: Retrieves a repository's community profile metrics, including health percentage and the presence of key community files (e.g., README, LICENSE).

tools.name: Get contextual information for a user
tools.description: Gets contextual hovercard information for a GitHub user; `subject_type` and `subject_id` can be jointly provided for more specific details.

tools.name: Get a custom deployment protection rule
tools.description: Retrieves a specific custom deployment protection rule (used by GitHub Apps for external validation or manual approval of deployments) for a given environment in a repository.

tools.name: Get default attributes for a codespace
tools.description: Get pre-flight data (e.g., default location, devcontainer path) for creating a Codespace in a given repository (must exist and be accessible), optionally for a specific Git ref.

tools.name: Get default workflow permissions for an organization
tools.description: Gets the default GITHUB_TOKEN workflow permissions and settings for a GitHub organization.

tools.name: Get default workflow permissions for a repository
tools.description: Gets the default workflow permissions (`read` or `write`) for the GITHUB_TOKEN and whether it can approve pull request reviews in an existing and accessible repository.

tools.name: Get a diff of the dependencies between commits
tools.description: Gets the dependency diff between two Git revisions in a repository, where 'basehead' specifies the revisions and 'name' can optionally scope to a specific manifest file.

tools.name: Get a deploy key
tools.description: Gets a specific deploy key, identified by its `key_id`, for the GitHub repository specified by `owner` and `repo`.

tools.name: Get a deployment
tools.description: Gets a specific deployment by ID from a repository, provided the repository and deployment ID exist.

tools.name: Get a deployment branch policy
tools.description: Retrieves a specific deployment branch policy for an environment in a repository, identified by its unique ID.

tools.name: Get a deployment status
tools.description: Retrieves a specific deployment status by its ID for a given deployment within a GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get details about a codespace export
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific export of a codespace.

tools.name: Get a discussion
tools.description: Fetches a specific discussion by its number from a team within an organization. This is a read-only action that retrieves discussion details including title, body, author information, comment count, and reactions. The authenticated user must be a member of the organization to access team discussions.

tools.name: Get a discussion comment
tools.description: Fetches a specific comment from a team discussion within a specific organization.

tools.name: Get emojis
tools.description: Lists all emojis available for use on GitHub, including custom and Unicode emojis.

tools.name: Get Enterprise Member Invitation
tools.description: Tool to look up a pending enterprise unaffiliated member invitation by invitee and enterprise. Use when you need to check the status of a specific invitation.

tools.name: Get feeds
tools.description: Fetches a list of available GitHub feed URLs for the authenticated user.

tools.name: Get a gist
tools.description: Fetches a specific GitHub gist by its `gist_id`, returning comprehensive details if the gist exists.

tools.name: Get a gist comment
tools.description: Retrieves a specific Gist comment by its ID and the Gist's ID.

tools.name: Get a gist revision
tools.description: Retrieves a specific revision of a gist.

tools.name: Get GitHub Actions cache usage for an organization
tools.description: Retrieves total GitHub Actions cache usage statistics for an organization, including active cache count and size across all repositories.

tools.name: Get github actions cache usage for a repository
tools.description: Retrieves the total count of active GitHub Actions caches and their combined size in bytes for a specified repository.

tools.name: Get github actions permissions for an organization
tools.description: Gets the GitHub Actions permissions for a specified organization, detailing repository enablement and allowed actions policies.

tools.name: Get GitHub Actions permissions for a repository
tools.description: Gets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for a repository, including its enabled status and the scope of allowed actions.

tools.name: Get billing premium request usage
tools.description: Tool to get billing premium request usage report for a GitHub user. Use when you need to retrieve usage data for premium features like Copilot or AI models.

tools.name: Get billing usage summary for user
tools.description: Tool to retrieve billing usage summary for a GitHub user account. Use when tracking usage costs, monitoring billing details, or generating usage reports for a specific user. Note: This endpoint only returns data for the past 24 months and is currently in public preview.

tools.name: Get github meta information
tools.description: Fetches GitHub's publicly available metadata, useful for configuring network security policies or IP allow-listing.

tools.name: Get github pages build
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific GitHub Pages build for a repository, which must have GitHub Pages enabled.

tools.name: Get a gitignore template
tools.description: Retrieves a specific .gitignore template from GitHub by its name, which must be an existing template in GitHub's collection.

tools.name: Get a global security advisory
tools.description: Retrieve a global GitHub security advisory by its GHSA identifier. Use this to get detailed information about vulnerabilities including severity, affected packages, CVSS scores, and references.

tools.name: Fetch GraphQL Node
tools.description: Tool to fetch any GitHub object by its global node ID. Use when you have a node_id and need to retrieve the corresponding object details.

tools.name: Get GraphQL rate limit
tools.description: Tool to retrieve the authenticated client's GitHub GraphQL API rate limit information. Use when you need to check remaining quota, current usage, or reset time for GraphQL requests.

tools.name: Get information about a sarif upload
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information, including processing status and results URL, about a specific SARIF (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format) upload for a repository, uniquely identified by its sarif_id.

tools.name: Get interaction restrictions for an organization
tools.description: Retrieves interaction restrictions for an organization, showing which GitHub user types can interact with its public repositories and when restrictions expire; returns an empty response if no restrictions are set.

tools.name: Get interaction restrictions for a repository
tools.description: Retrieves active interaction restrictions for a repository, detailing which user groups are limited from activities like commenting or creating pull requests, and when these restrictions expire.

tools.name: Get interaction restrictions for public repositories
tools.description: Retrieves currently active interaction restrictions for the authenticated user's public repositories.

tools.name: Get a label
tools.description: Retrieves a specific label by its name from a specified GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get large files
tools.description: Lists files larger than 100MB identified during a previous source import for the specified repository. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

tools.name: Get latest pages build
tools.description: Retrieves information about the most recent GitHub Pages build for a repository, which must exist, be accessible, have GitHub Pages enabled, and have at least one prior build.

tools.name: Get a license
tools.description: Call this action to retrieve comprehensive details for a specific software license recognized by GitHub, using its unique license key.

tools.name: Get License (GraphQL)
tools.description: Tool to look up an open source license by its SPDX ID using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to fetch license details for compliance tracking or repository setup.

tools.name: Get octocat
tools.description: Fetches an ASCII art representation of GitHub's Octocat, suitable for text-based displays.

tools.name: Customize OIDC subject claim template
tools.description: Retrieves the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim customization template for a repository, which defines the `sub` claim structure in OIDC tokens for GitHub Actions workflows; returns the default configuration if no customization is applied.

tools.name: Get allowed actions and workflows for an org
tools.description: Gets the list of allowed actions and reusable workflows for an organization when the 'allowed_actions' policy is set to 'selected'.

tools.name: Get organization audit log
tools.description: Tool to retrieve an organization's audit log events for security and compliance investigations. Use when tracking organizational changes, security audits, or compliance monitoring. Requires organization owner permissions and read:audit_log scope.

tools.name: Get a custom property for an organization
tools.description: Retrieves the definition (schema) of a specific, existing custom property for an organization.

tools.name: Get organization project item
tools.description: Tool to get an item from an organization-owned GitHub Projects V2 project. Use when you need to retrieve details about a specific project item including its content, fields, timestamps, and creator information.

tools.name: Get organization public key for Codespaces secrets
tools.description: Retrieves an organization's public key, which must be used to encrypt secret values before they are configured for Codespaces.

tools.name: Fetch Public Key For Secret Encryption
tools.description: Retrieves the public key for an existing GitHub organization, required for encrypting Dependabot secrets. Requires admin:org scope.

tools.name: Get org dev environment secret safely
tools.description: Retrieves metadata for a specific secret available to an organization's GitHub Codespaces without exposing its encrypted value.

tools.name: Get custom OIDC subject claim template
tools.description: Retrieves the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim customization template for a GitHub organization, which defines how the `sub` claim in OIDC tokens for workflows is constructed.

tools.name: Get a dns health check for github pages
tools.description: Retrieves the DNS health check status (e.g., CNAME/A records, HTTPS) for a GitHub Pages site; the check may be pending (HTTP 202) on initial calls or after site changes.

tools.name: Get a GitHub Pages site
tools.description: Retrieves information for a GitHub Pages site, which must be enabled for the repository.

tools.name: Get page views
tools.description: Retrieves page view statistics for a repository over the last 14 days, including total views, unique visitors, and a daily or weekly breakdown.

tools.name: Get pending deployments for a workflow run
tools.description: Retrieves pending deployment environments for a specific workflow run that are awaiting approval due to protection rules.

tools.name: Get project permission for a user
tools.description: Retrieves permission information for a GitHub project (classic) collaborator. Returns the permission level and full user object for a collaborator on a GitHub project (classic). The permission returned can be admin, write, read, or none.

tools.name: Get public key for secret encryption
tools.description: Retrieves a repository's public key for encrypting GitHub Codespaces secrets; requires `repo` scope or equivalent read access to codespaces secrets for private repositories.

tools.name: Get public key for the authenticated user
tools.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's public key for encrypting GitHub Codespaces secrets. The returned key must be used with libsodium sealed box encryption before creating or updating secrets via the API.

tools.name: Get pull request review protection
tools.description: Retrieves the pull request review protection settings for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, if such protection is configured.

tools.name: Get rate limit status for the authenticated user
tools.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's current GitHub API rate limit status, including usage and limits across different resource categories.

tools.name: Get Raw Repository Content
tools.description: Tool to fetch raw file content from a GitHub repository. Returns raw bytes in whatever format the file uses (JSON, CSV, binary, etc.) — parsing and validation are the caller's responsibility. Ensure owner, repo, ref, and path are consistent when combining with other GitHub tools to avoid mismatched-ref errors.

tools.name: Get allowed actions and workflows for a repository
tools.description: Gets the settings for allowed actions and reusable workflows in a repository. Requires the repository's `allowed_actions` policy to be set to `selected` (returns 409 if set to `all` or `local_only`).

tools.name: Get repository development environment secret
tools.description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for a specific, existing development environment secret (Codespaces secret) in a repository, without exposing its value.

tools.name: Get repository attestations
tools.description: Tool to retrieve attestations by subject digest from a GitHub repository. Use when you need to verify or check attestations for a specific artifact identified by its SHA256 digest.

tools.name: Get repository clones
tools.description: Retrieves the total number of clones and a breakdown of clone activity (daily or weekly) for a specified repository over the preceding 14 days.

tools.name: Get repository content
tools.description: Retrieves a file's Base64 encoded content or lists a directory's contents from a GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get Repository via GraphQL
tools.description: Tool to lookup a repository by owner and name using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need comprehensive repository information.

tools.name: Get Repository Owner
tools.description: Tool to lookup a repository owner (User or Organization) by login using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to determine whether a login is a user or organization and retrieve basic profile information.

tools.name: Get repository permissions for a user
tools.description: Retrieves a specific user's permission level ('admin', 'write', 'read', or 'none') for a given repository, where 'maintain' role is reported as 'write' and 'triage' as 'read'.

tools.name: Retrieve repo public key for encryption
tools.description: Gets a repository's public key, used to encrypt secrets for Dependabot.

tools.name: Get repository secret securely
tools.description: Retrieves metadata for an existing Dependabot secret in a repository, without exposing its encrypted value.

tools.name: Get repository security advisory
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a repository security advisory using its GHSA identifier. Use when you need detailed information about a specific security vulnerability.

tools.name: Get a delivery for a repository webhook
tools.description: Retrieves a specific delivery for a repository webhook, identified by its `hook_id` and `delivery_id`.

tools.name: Get rules for a branch
tools.description: Retrieves all active rules for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, excluding rules in 'evaluate' or 'disabled' status.

tools.name: Get Organization Dependabot Secret
tools.description: Gets a single organization Dependabot secret's metadata (name, timestamps, visibility) without revealing its encrypted value. Requires admin:org scope.

tools.name: Get status checks protection
tools.description: Retrieves the status check protection settings for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, if status check protection is enabled for it.

tools.name: Get team membership for a user
tools.description: Retrieves a user's role and membership status within a specific team in an organization. Returns membership details including role ('member' or 'maintainer') and state ('active' or 'pending'). If the user is not a member of the team, returns is_member=False with a descriptive message. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization member with visibility into the team.

tools.name: Get teams with access to the protected branch
tools.description: Lists teams with explicit push access to a protected branch, provided team restrictions are configured in the branch's protection settings; returns an empty list otherwise.

tools.name: Get the authenticated user
tools.description: Gets the profile information for the currently authenticated GitHub user, including potentially private details based on user settings.

tools.name: Get the combined status for a specific reference
tools.description: Retrieves the aggregated commit status (e.g., success, failure, pending) from all checks for a specific reference (SHA, branch, or tag) in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get the hourly commit count for each day
tools.description: Retrieves the 'punch card' data, showing hourly commit counts for each day of the week for an existing and accessible repository.

tools.name: Get the last year of commit activity
tools.description: Fetches weekly commit totals and daily commit counts for the last 52 weeks for a specified GitHub repository.

tools.name: Get the latest release
tools.description: Fetches the latest official (non-prerelease, non-draft) release for a GitHub repository; requires at least one such published release.

tools.name: Get the license for a repository
tools.description: Retrieves the license file and its details for a repository, optionally from a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA).

tools.name: Get the review history for a workflow run
tools.description: Retrieves the detailed approval history for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository, detailing each review's environment, state, reviewer, and comments, to track the approval process for workflows, particularly automated deployments.

tools.name: Get the status of a GitHub Pages deployment
tools.description: Retrieves the status of a specific GitHub Pages deployment for a repository, which must have GitHub Pages enabled.

tools.name: Get the weekly commit activity
tools.description: Fetches the weekly commit activity (additions and deletions per week) for a repository over the past year; best for repositories with under 10,000 commits.

tools.name: Get the weekly commit count
tools.description: Retrieves the weekly commit count for a repository, detailing commits by the owner and all contributors over the last 52 weeks; GitHub may return a 202 status or an empty response if statistics are being computed.

tools.name: Get the Zen of GitHub
tools.description: Retrieves a random quote from GitHub's 'Zen of GitHub' collection, reflecting GitHub's design philosophies and offering humorous insights, useful for displaying GitHub wisdom or a lighthearted message.

tools.name: Get top referral paths
tools.description: Fetches the top 10 most viewed content paths for a repository from the last 14 days.

tools.name: Get top referral sources
tools.description: Fetches the top 10 websites that referred traffic to a repository within the last 14 days.

tools.name: Get user by ID
tools.description: Retrieves a GitHub user's profile information by their unique numeric account ID. Use when you know the user's numeric ID rather than their username.

tools.name: Get user project item
tools.description: Tool to get an item from a user-owned project in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to retrieve details about a specific item (issue, pull request, or draft issue) in a user's project board.

tools.name: Get user attestations by subject digest
tools.description: Tool to get attestations by subject digest for a GitHub user. Use when you need to retrieve attestation data for a specific artifact or resource identified by its subject digest.

tools.name: Get user's projects v2
tools.description: Tool to list GitHub Projects v2 for a specified user. Use when you need to retrieve all projects owned by a user with pagination support.

tools.name: Get users with access to the protected branch
tools.description: Lists users with explicit push access to a protected branch, provided its protection rule restricts pushes to specific users.

tools.name: Get Viewer via GraphQL
tools.description: Tool to retrieve the authenticated user's profile information via GitHub GraphQL. Use when working with GraphQL queries or need viewer details in GraphQL format.

tools.name: Get workflow external access level
tools.description: Gets the access level settings for a private repository, determining how workflows outside this repository can use its actions and reusable workflows.

tools.name: Get a job for a workflow run
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific job within a GitHub Actions workflow run, given its `job_id` which must be valid for the specified repository's workflow.

tools.name: Get workflow run usage
tools.description: Gets the billable time, in milliseconds, for a specific workflow run, detailing time spent on various operating systems.

tools.name: Get workflow usage
tools.description: Gets the billable time (in milliseconds, broken down by runner OS) for a specific workflow within a repository for the current billing cycle.

tools.name: Check if repo starred by auth user
tools.description: Use to determine if the authenticated user has starred a specific GitHub repository, which is confirmed by an HTTP 204 status (resulting in an empty dictionary in the response data); the action fails (e.g., HTTP 404) if the repository is not starred or does not exist.

tools.name: List accepted assignments for an assignment
tools.description: Lists accepted assignments (student repositories created after acceptance) for an existing GitHub Classroom assignment, identified by its unique `assignment_id`.

tools.name: List repositories accessible via GitHub App installation
tools.description: Lists repositories that the authenticated user can access through a specific GitHub App installation. Returns repositories where the user has explicit :read, :write, or :admin permission through the installation. The response includes permission details for each repository. Prerequisites: - Requires a GitHub App user access token (not a standard OAuth token) - Use GITHUB_LIST_APP_INSTALLATIONS first to get valid installation IDs

tools.name: List app installations for user token
tools.description: Lists GitHub App installations accessible to the authenticated user via their access token, including installation details, permissions, and repository access.

tools.name: Get apps with access to the protected branch
tools.description: Lists GitHub Apps with push access to a repository's protected branch.

tools.name: List artifacts for a repository
tools.description: Lists GitHub Actions workflow artifacts for a specified repository, which must exist.

tools.name: List assignees
tools.description: Lists users who can be assigned to issues in a repository, typically those with push access.

tools.name: List assignments for a classroom
tools.description: Lists all assignments for a given GitHub Classroom `classroom_id`; the classroom must exist and be accessible.

tools.name: List attestation repositories
tools.description: Tool to list attestation repositories for an organization. Use when you need to discover which repositories in an organization have attestations. Attestations provide verifiable metadata about software artifacts.

tools.name: List available machine types for a repository
tools.description: Lists machine types available for GitHub Codespaces in a repository, optionally using a Git ref to check compatibility based on prebuild availability and devcontainer configurations.

tools.name: List branches
tools.description: Lists branches for an existing GitHub repository, with an option to filter by protection status.

tools.name: List branches for head commit
tools.description: Lists branches in an accessible repository where the provided commit SHA is the head, useful for identifying development lines based on that commit.

tools.name: List check run annotations
tools.description: Lists annotations for a specific check run in a GitHub repository, detailing issues like errors or warnings on particular code lines.

tools.name: List check runs for a git ref
tools.description: List GitHub check runs for a commit SHA, branch, or tag to assess CI status and conclusions. Use when you need reliable CI pass/fail signals beyond commit metadata.

tools.name: List check runs in a check suite
tools.description: Lists check runs for a specific check suite in a GitHub repository, optionally filtering by check name or status.

tools.name: List check suites for a git reference
tools.description: Lists check suites for a Git reference (commit SHA, branch, or tag) in a repository, optionally filtering by GitHub App ID or check run name.

tools.name: List child teams
tools.description: Lists the immediate child teams of a parent team within an organization.

tools.name: List classrooms
tools.description: Lists GitHub Classrooms to which the authenticated user has administrative access.

tools.name: List codeowners errors
tools.description: Lists syntax errors in a repository's CODEOWNERS file, which must be located at the root, `.github/`, or `docs/` directory for the specified ref.

tools.name: List CodeQL databases for a repository
tools.description: Lists all CodeQL databases for a repository where CodeQL analysis has been previously run and completed.

tools.name: List code scanning alerts for an organization
tools.description: Lists code scanning alerts for a GitHub organization; use EITHER `tool_name` OR `tool_guid` if filtering by tool, not both.

tools.name: List code scanning alerts for a repository
tools.description: Lists code scanning alerts for a repository, optionally filtering by tool (which must have produced scan results for the repository), Git reference, state, or severity.

tools.name: List code scanning analyses for a repository
tools.description: Lists code scanning analyses for an existing repository, optionally filtering by tool (name or GUID), Git reference, or SARIF ID.

tools.name: Get all codes of conduct
tools.description: Retrieves all available codes of conduct from GitHub, often used to select one for a repository.

tools.name: List Codes of Conduct (GraphQL)
tools.description: Tool to fetch all available codes of conduct using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to retrieve the complete list of codes of conduct that can be applied to repositories.

tools.name: List codespaces for a user in organization
tools.description: Lists all GitHub Codespaces owned by a specified member of a given organization.

tools.name: List codespaces for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists GitHub Codespaces for the authenticated user, optionally filtering by repository ID and supporting pagination.

tools.name: List codespaces for the organization
tools.description: Lists active/pending GitHub Codespaces for an existing organization; admins list all, members list their own.

tools.name: List comment changes across organization
tools.description: Tool to list issue and PR comment changes across an organization's repositories efficiently. Use when monitoring comment activity without per-PR/per-issue polling. Filters organization events to return only comment-related events (IssueCommentEvent, PullRequestReviewCommentEvent, CommitCommentEvent). Note: Events are limited to the past 30 days and up to 300 events per timeline. Use ETag header for efficient polling to avoid rate limits.

tools.name: List comments for a pull request review
tools.description: Lists all comments for a specific review on a GitHub pull request.

tools.name: List commit comments
tools.description: Retrieves all comments for a specific commit in a GitHub repository, supporting pagination.

tools.name: List commit comments for a repository
tools.description: Lists all commit comments for a specified repository, which must exist and be accessible.

tools.name: List commits
tools.description: Lists commits for a GitHub repository. Requires 'owner' (username/org) and 'repo' (repository name) parameters. Optionally filter by SHA/branch, path, author, committer, or date range.

tools.name: List commits on a pull request
tools.description: Lists commits for a pull request; requires the repository and pull request to exist and be accessible, and supports pagination.

tools.name: Get all commonly used licenses
tools.description: Retrieves a list of commonly used software licenses from GitHub, optionally filtering for 'featured' licenses whose specific selection criteria by GitHub may vary.

tools.name: Get all custom properties for an organization
tools.description: Gets the schema definitions for all custom properties configured for an organization, not the specific values assigned to repositories.

tools.name: Get all custom property values for a repository
tools.description: Gets all custom property values for a repository. Custom properties are defined at the organization level, so this endpoint works with organization-owned repositories. Returns an empty array if no custom properties are set.

tools.name: List custom property values for organization repositories
tools.description: Lists repositories in an organization with their custom property values. Requires 'Custom properties' organization permission (read).

tools.name: List deliveries for an organization webhook
tools.description: Retrieves a list of webhook deliveries for a specific webhook in an organization, allowing inspection of delivery history and details.

tools.name: List deliveries for a repository webhook
tools.description: Retrieves delivery attempts for a specific repository webhook to inspect its history; ensure the webhook ID exists.

tools.name: List deploy keys
tools.description: Lists deploy SSH keys for a specified repository; the repository must exist.

tools.name: List deployment branch policies
tools.description: Lists all deployment branch policies for a specified environment in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: List deployments
tools.description: Lists deployments for a specified repository; repository must exist.

tools.name: List deployment statuses
tools.description: Lists all statuses for a given deployment in a repository.

tools.name: List discussion comments
tools.description: Lists all comments for a specific team discussion within an organization. Note: Team discussions are deprecated as of 2023-11-28. Consider using GitHub Discussions instead. This endpoint requires the authenticated user to be a member of the organization and have access to the team's discussions.

tools.name: List discussions
tools.description: Lists discussions for a specific team within an organization, with options for sorting, pagination, and filtering by pinned status. Note: Team discussions are deprecated as of 2023-11-28. Consider using GitHub Discussions instead. This endpoint requires the authenticated user to be a member of the organization and have access to the team's discussions.

tools.name: List email addresses for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists all email addresses for the authenticated user, including their primary status, verification status, and visibility. This action retrieves the complete list of email addresses associated with the authenticated user, providing important details such as which email is primary and whether the emails are verified.

tools.name: List environment custom deployment rules
tools.description: Lists all custom deployment protection rule integrations for a repository environment; the `environment_name` must be URL-encoded.

tools.name: List environments
tools.description: Retrieves all deployment environments for a specified repository, which are used to configure protection rules and secrets for different software lifecycle stages.

tools.name: List environment secrets
tools.description: Lists the names and metadata (not values) of secrets for a specified, existing environment within an existing GitHub repository.

tools.name: List environment variables
tools.description: Lists all environment variables, which are plaintext key-value pairs for GitHub Actions workflows, for a specified environment within a GitHub repository.

tools.name: List events for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists public events for the specified GitHub user, or private events if authenticated as that user, in reverse chronological order.

tools.name: List events received by the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists events a specific GitHub user received from followed users and watched repositories; returns private events if authenticated for `username`, otherwise public.

tools.name: List followers of a user
tools.description: Lists followers for a specified, existing GitHub user.

tools.name: List followers of the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists users following the authenticated GitHub user, returning an empty list if the user has no followers.

tools.name: List forks
tools.description: Lists forks for a specified repository, which must exist, with options for sorting and pagination.

tools.name: List gist comments
tools.description: Lists comments for a specified GitHub Gist.

tools.name: List gist commits
tools.description: Lists all commits for a specified gist.

tools.name: List gist forks
tools.description: Lists all forks for a given GitHub gist ID.

tools.name: List gists for a user
tools.description: _lists public gists for a specified GitHub user._

tools.name: List gists for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists gists for the authenticated user, with optional filtering by update time and pagination.

tools.name: List github actions caches for a repository
tools.description: Lists GitHub Actions caches for a repository, with options to filter by Git reference or cache key, and to sort and paginate results.

tools.name: List GitHub Pages builds
tools.description: Lists GitHub Pages builds for a repository; GitHub Pages must be enabled on the repository for builds to be listed.

tools.name: Get all gitignore templates
tools.description: Retrieves all available .gitignore template names from GitHub (e.g., 'Python', 'Node', 'Java'), used for generating .gitignore files.

tools.name: List global security advisories
tools.description: Lists GitHub's global security advisories, filterable by various attributes including ID, type, ecosystem, severity, and dates.

tools.name: List GPG keys for a user
tools.description: Tool to list GPG keys for a GitHub user. Use when you need to fetch the public GPG keys associated with a specific GitHub user account.

tools.name: List installation requests for the authenticated app
tools.description: Lists pending installation requests made by users or organizations for the authenticated GitHub App.

tools.name: List instances of a code scanning alert
tools.description: Lists all instances of a specific code scanning alert, optionally filtered by Git ref; requires code scanning to be enabled on the repository.

tools.name: List issue comments
tools.description: Lists comments for a specified issue in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: List issue comments for a repository
tools.description: Lists issue comments, including those on pull requests, for an accessible repository.

tools.name: List issue dependencies blocked by
tools.description: Tool to list dependencies an issue is blocked by. Use when you need to retrieve all issues that are blocking a specific issue from being closed or completed.

tools.name: List issue dependencies blocking
tools.description: Tool to list dependencies an issue is blocking. Use when you need to see which issues are blocked by a specific issue.

tools.name: List issue events
tools.description: Retrieves a list of all events for a specific issue within a GitHub repository.

tools.name: List issue events for a repository
tools.description: Lists all issue events (e.g., closed, reopened, assigned) for a specified repository.

tools.name: List issues for user
tools.description: Lists GitHub issues for the authenticated user across visible repositories, with filtering by user relationship, state, labels, an ISO 8601 `since` timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ), and sorting options; pull requests are typically included.

tools.name: List issue types
tools.description: List all issue types configured for a GitHub organization. Requires read:org scope for OAuth tokens.

tools.name: List jobs for a workflow run
tools.description: Lists jobs for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: List jobs for a workflow run attempt
tools.description: Lists jobs, including details like ID, status, and steps, for a specific attempt of a GitHub Actions workflow run.

tools.name: List labels for an issue
tools.description: Lists all labels for a specified issue in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: List labels for a repository
tools.description: Retrieves all labels for a specified, existing GitHub repository.

tools.name: List labels for a self-hosted runner for a repository
tools.description: Lists all labels assigned to a specific self-hosted runner registered with the given repository.

tools.name: List labels for issues in a milestone
tools.description: Lists all labels for issues within a specific milestone in a repository.

tools.name: List labels for a self-hosted runner for an organization
tools.description: Lists all labels assigned to a specific self-hosted runner within a GitHub organization, which are used to route workflows.

tools.name: List Licenses (GraphQL)
tools.description: Tool to fetch all known open source licenses using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to retrieve the complete list of licenses that can be applied to repositories.

tools.name: List machine types for a codespace
tools.description: Lists available machine types for a specific, accessible codespace, enabling it to be transitioned to a new hardware configuration.

tools.name: List matching references
tools.description: Lists all Git references (branches or tags) in a repository that start with the provided partial reference path (e.g., `heads/my-feature` or `tags/v1.2`).

tools.name: List milestones
tools.description: Lists milestones, which track progress for groups of issues and pull requests, for an existing repository, allowing filtering by state and sorting.

tools.name: List notifications for authenticated user
tools.description: Tool to list notification threads for the authenticated user with efficient polling support. Use when you need to fetch the user's GitHub notification inbox with filters like unread/participating/since. Returns notification threads plus polling headers (Last-Modified, ETag, X-Poll-Interval) for efficient polling. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a Personal Access Token (classic) with 'notifications' or 'repo' scope. It does NOT work with GitHub App tokens or fine-grained personal access tokens. If the token type is incompatible, a clear error message will be returned.

tools.name: List notifications for the authenticated user (Deprecated)
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Use GITHUB_LIST_NOTIFICATIONS instead. Lists notifications for the authenticated user, sorted by most recent update, with filtering and pagination options.

tools.name: List org level codespaces secrets
tools.description: Lists all Codespaces secrets available for a specified organization, which must exist.

tools.name: List organization events for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists public events in a specified GitHub organization for the authenticated user's organization dashboard. Returns activities like repository pushes, pull requests, issues, forks, and other public events within the organization. The authenticated user must be a member of the organization to see events.

tools.name: List organization members
tools.description: Lists public and concealed members of a GitHub organization; viewing concealed members requires authenticated user to be an organization member.

tools.name: List organization memberships for authenticated user
tools.description: List organization memberships for authenticated user

tools.name: List organization projects
tools.description: Lists projects (V2) for a specified GitHub organization, with optional search query and cursor-based pagination.

tools.name: List organization repositories
tools.description: Retrieves a list of repositories for a specified GitHub organization, allowing filtering by type and sorting.

tools.name: Get all organization roles for an organization
tools.description: Lists all custom organization roles for an existing GitHub organization.

tools.name: List organizations
tools.description: Lists GitHub organizations for the authenticated user, sorted by ID in ascending order.

tools.name: List organization secrets
tools.description: Lists GitHub Actions secrets available for a specified organization.

tools.name: List organizations for a user
tools.description: Lists public organizations for a specified GitHub user; the `username` must be a valid GitHub handle.

tools.name: List organizations for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists organizations the authenticated GitHub user is a member of, returning details for each organization.

tools.name: List organization variables
tools.description: Lists all GitHub Actions variables for a specified organization.

tools.name: List organization webhooks
tools.description: Lists all webhooks for a specified GitHub organization; the organization must exist.

tools.name: List organization fine-grained permissions
tools.description: Lists all fine-grained permissions available in a GitHub organization for use in custom organization roles. These permissions define granular access controls that can be combined when creating custom roles. Note: This endpoint requires GitHub Enterprise Cloud with the custom organization roles feature enabled. The authenticated user must be an administrator or have the 'read_organization_custom_org_role' permission.

tools.name: List organization issues assigned to the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists issues for the authenticated user within a specified GitHub organization, with options to filter by involvement type, state, labels, and to sort results.

tools.name: List package versions for an organization package
tools.description: Lists all versions for a specified package, if it exists and is owned by the given GitHub organization.

tools.name: List organization project view items
tools.description: Tool to list items for an organization project view. Use when you need to retrieve all items from a specific view within a GitHub organization's Projects V2 project. Returns a list of project items with their content, metadata, and field values.

tools.name: List organization repositories with Github Actions enabled
tools.description: Lists the selected repositories that are enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. This endpoint only returns results when the organization's 'enabled_repositories' permission policy is configured to 'selected'. Requires admin:org scope and organization admin permissions.

tools.name: List org resource access tokens
tools.description: Lists approved fine-grained personal access tokens (PATs) with access to resources in a GitHub organization, optionally filtering by owner, repository, permissions, or last usage time. AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (read). Standard personal access tokens CANNOT use this endpoint. The GitHub App must also have access to the organization.

tools.name: List org resources with personal tokens
tools.description: Lists requests from organization members to access organization resources with fine-grained personal access tokens (pending, approved, or denied). Note: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'organization_personal_access_token_requests:read' permissions; it cannot be used with personal access tokens.

tools.name: List organization Dependabot secrets without values
tools.description: Lists all Dependabot secrets (metadata like names, creation/update timestamps, but not their encrypted values) for a specified organization, which must exist.

tools.name: List outside collaborators for an organization
tools.description: Lists outside collaborators for a GitHub organization, with options to filter (e.g., by 2FA status) and paginate results.

tools.name: List owned package versions
tools.description: Lists all versions for an existing package owned by the authenticated user, identified by its type and name.

tools.name: List packages for an organization
tools.description: Lists GitHub Packages for an organization, noting specific interpretations for package_type and visibility parameters.

tools.name: List packages for a user
tools.description: Lists packages for a specified GitHub user, filterable by package type and visibility.

tools.name: List packages for the authenticated user's namespace
tools.description: Lists packages of a specific type and visibility within the authenticated user's namespace on GitHub.

tools.name: List package versions for a package owned by a user
tools.description: Lists all versions of a public package owned by a specific GitHub user, identified by package type, package name, and username.

tools.name: List pending team invitations
tools.description: Lists all pending membership invitations for a specified team within an organization. The authenticated user must be an organization member with the read:org scope. The response includes invitation details such as the invitee's login/email, the role they're being invited to, and who sent the invitation. The 'role' field indicates the type of invitation: - 'direct_member': Regular organization member - 'admin': Organization administrator - 'billing_manager': Billing manager role - 'hiring_manager': Hiring manager role - 'reinstate': Reinstating a previous member Note: If the invitee is not a GitHub member, the 'login' field will be null and only the 'email' field will be populated.

tools.name: List project cards (Classic - Deprecated)
tools.description: Lists all project cards for a given column_id in GitHub Projects (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns a list of project cards including: id, node_id, url, note (or null for linked cards), creator info, content_url (for issue/PR cards), column_url, project_url, timestamps, and archived status.

tools.name: List project collaborators (classic)
tools.description: Lists collaborators for a GitHub Project (classic) by project ID. IMPORTANT: This action uses the Projects (classic) REST API which was sunset on April 1, 2025 for GitHub.com. The API may still be available on GitHub Enterprise Server versions before 3.17. For GitHub.com users: GitHub Projects (classic) has been replaced by the new Projects experience. The new Projects does not have a REST API for listing collaborators - use the GitHub web interface to manage project collaborators instead. You must be an organization owner or a project admin to list collaborators.

tools.name: List project columns
tools.description: Lists all of a GitHub project's columns (e.g., 'To Do', 'In Progress'); project_id must identify a valid, accessible project. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects (classic) REST API. The Projects (classic) API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return an empty columns list with an informative message for projects that no longer exist or when the API returns 404.

tools.name: List project fields for user
tools.description: Tool to list all fields in a GitHub Projects V2 project for a specific user. Use when you need to see available fields in a project.

tools.name: List project items for user
tools.description: Tool to list items for a user-owned GitHub project. Use when you need to retrieve all items (issues, pull requests, draft issues) from a specific project belonging to a user.

tools.name: List items for user project view
tools.description: Tool to list items for a user's GitHub Projects (v2) view. Use when you need to retrieve items from a specific view of a user's project. Supports pagination and field filtering.

tools.name: List public email addresses for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists the public email addresses for the authenticated user.

tools.name: List public events
tools.description: Lists public GitHub events, which may be delayed by up to 5 minutes, with support for pagination.

tools.name: List public events for a network of repositories
tools.description: Retrieves public events (up to 90 days old, newest first) for a GitHub repository network, including the repository and its forks.

tools.name: List public events for a user
tools.description: Retrieves a list of public events for a specified GitHub user, in reverse chronological order.

tools.name: List public events received by a user
tools.description: Lists public events for a specified GitHub user (e.g., activities in repositories they watch or are involved in); the target user's profile must be public, and if blocked by the authenticated user, a 404 error is returned.

tools.name: List public gists
tools.description: Lists public gists from GitHub, optionally filtering by a 'since' timestamp and supporting pagination; results are generally newest first.

tools.name: List public keys for a user
tools.description: Lists the verified public SSH keys for a specified GitHub user.

tools.name: List public organization events
tools.description: Lists public events for a specified GitHub organization, which must exist. This action retrieves the events that are publicly accessible for the organization specified. It returns a paginated list of events with details about each event.

tools.name: List public organization members
tools.description: Lists users who have publicly declared their membership in a specified, existing GitHub organization.

tools.name: List public repositories
tools.description: Lists all public repositories on GitHub; use the `since` parameter with a repository ID from a previous result for pagination.

tools.name: List pull requests
tools.description: Lists pull requests for a GitHub repository. REQUIRES 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters - both are MANDATORY and must be provided in every call. Does NOT support filtering by assignee or date range - use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead for searching PRs by assignee, author, or across multiple repositories.

tools.name: List pull requests associated with a commit
tools.description: Lists pull requests for a commit; returns merged PRs that introduced the commit if on the default branch, or open PRs including the commit if on other branches.

tools.name: List pull requests files
tools.description: Lists all files (including additions, modifications, and removals) associated with a specific pull request in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: List reactions for a commit comment
tools.description: Lists reactions for a specific commit comment; this is a read-only operation.

tools.name: List reactions for an issue
tools.description: Lists reactions for a specific, existing issue within an accessible GitHub repository, optionally filtering by content type.

tools.name: List reactions for an issue comment
tools.description: Lists reactions for a specific issue comment in a GitHub repository, optionally filtering by content type.

tools.name: List reactions for a pull request review comment
tools.description: Lists reactions for a pull request review comment in a repository, optionally filtering by reaction type.

tools.name: List reactions for a release
tools.description: Lists all reactions, or optionally filters reactions by a specific content type, for a given GitHub release.

tools.name: List reactions for a team discussion
tools.description: Lists reactions for an existing team discussion within an organization. This endpoint allows you to retrieve all reactions (emoji responses) made to a specific team discussion. You can optionally filter by reaction type. Note: OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the `read:discussion` scope to use this endpoint.

tools.name: List reactions for a team discussion comment
tools.description: Lists reactions for a specific comment in a team discussion within an organization, optionally filtering by content type.

tools.name: List release assets
tools.description: Lists assets (e.g., compiled binaries, source code archives) for a specific GitHub release, identified by `release_id` which must be valid for an existing release in the repository.

tools.name: List releases
tools.description: Retrieves a list of all releases (published, draft, and prerelease) for a specified repository.

tools.name: List repo access by token
tools.description: Lists repositories in an organization that a fine-grained personal access token has requested access to. Only GitHub Apps can use this endpoint. Requires 'Personal access token requests' organization permissions (read).

tools.name: List repo codespaces for authenticated user
tools.description: Lists Codespaces the authenticated user can access within a specified, existing repository.

tools.name: List repo dev container configs for user
tools.description: Lists the `devcontainer.json` configurations available in a specified repository for use with GitHub Codespaces.

tools.name: List authenticated user's repository invitations
tools.description: Lists all pending repository invitations for the authenticated user.

tools.name: List repository notifications for the authenticated user
tools.description: Retrieves notifications for the authenticated user from a specified repository, to which the user must have access.

tools.name: List repo secrets without values
tools.description: Lists all Codespaces secrets available in a specific repository, without their encrypted values.

tools.name: List repos accessible to app installation
tools.description: Lists repositories a GitHub App installation can access. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token (ghs_* prefix) for authentication. OAuth/user access tokens (gho_*) or personal access tokens are not supported and will result in a 403 Forbidden error. Returns 'total_count' and 'repositories' array with pagination support.

tools.name: List repositories for a user
tools.description: Lists public repositories for a specified GitHub user, who must have an existing account.

tools.name: List repositories for the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists repositories for the authenticated user; using 'type' with 'visibility' or 'affiliation' API parameters (not in this model) can cause a 422 error.

tools.name: List repositories starred by a user
tools.description: Lists repositories a valid and existing GitHub user has starred.

tools.name: List repositories starred by the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists repositories the authenticated user has starred, optionally sorted and paginated, including star creation timestamps via 'application/vnd.github.star+json' media type.

tools.name: List repositories watched by a user
tools.description: Lists repositories a given GitHub user is watching; the username must be a valid and existing GitHub user handle.

tools.name: List repositories watched by the authenticated user
tools.description: Lists repositories the authenticated user is watching (subscribed to for notifications). This action retrieves a list of repositories that the authenticated user is currently watching. It allows the user to manage their watched repositories effectively.

tools.name: List repos with ghactions cache usage
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated list of GitHub Actions cache usage statistics for repositories within an existing GitHub organization.

tools.name: List repository activities
tools.description: Lists activities for a GitHub repository, ensuring the repository exists and is accessible.

tools.name: Get all autolinks of a repository
tools.description: Retrieves all autolinks (e.g., for JIRA issues) configured for a repository; requires admin permissions on the repository.

tools.name: List repository collaborators
tools.description: Lists collaborators for a specified repository, provided it exists and is accessible to the authenticated user.

tools.name: List repository contributors
tools.description: Lists contributors to a specified repository, sorted by number of contributions in descending order; the repository must exist and be accessible.

tools.name: List repository events
tools.description: Lists chronological events (e.g., code pushes, issue activities, PR actions, forks) for a specified, existing GitHub repository.

tools.name: List repository invitations
tools.description: Retrieves all pending (unaccepted or undeclined) collaboration invitations for a specified GitHub repository.

tools.name: List repository issues
tools.description: Lists issues (including pull requests) from a GitHub repository. Requires BOTH 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters (e.g., owner='octocat', repo='Hello-World' for github.com/octocat/Hello-World).

tools.name: List repository languages
tools.description: Lists the programming languages used in a GitHub repository, returning a byte count for each language.

tools.name: List repository organization secrets
tools.description: Lists names of organization-level secrets shared with the specified repository; actual secret values are not returned and visibility depends on token access.

tools.name: List repository organization variables
tools.description: Lists organization-level variables that are shared with a specific repository. The repository must belong to an organization (not a personal account).

tools.name: List repository projects
tools.description: Lists Projects (classic) associated with a specific repository. Note: This action lists GitHub Projects (classic), which is GitHub's legacy project management feature. If the repository has Projects (classic) disabled or uses the newer Projects V2, this will return an empty list. Most modern repositories use Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead of Projects (classic).

tools.name: Get all repository rulesets
tools.description: Retrieves all rulesets for a GitHub repository, which define conditions and actions for repository interactions (e.g., branch protections).

tools.name: List repository rule suites
